title: Lappula betpakdalensis
taxonomy: Lappula betpakdalensis is a species of flowering plant in the borage family (Boraginaceae). It is native to Kazakhstan. The plant is classified as a member of the genus Lappula, which includes several species of annual or biennial plants. Lappula betpakdalensis is often found growing in dry, sandy areas and is relatively rare in the wild. As a member of the Boraginaceae family, it is closely related to other plants in the same family, such as forget-me-nots and heliotrope.

Traditionally, Lappula betpakdalensis has been used in folk medicine for its purported health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce pain and inflammation, as well as protect against oxidative stress and cell damage.
